agent1_snippets.title: Pemphigus Vulgaris -- Treatment / Management -- Anti-CD20 monoclonal antibodies
agent1_snippets.content: Anti-CD20 monoclonal antibodies, such as rituximab and ofatumumab, are the preferred modality to use in conjunction with corticosteroids for first-line treatment in moderate-to-severe pemphigus. [59] Rituximab is an anti-CD20 monoclonal antibody that stops B lymphocytes from maturing into autoantibody-producing plasma cells. It is administered intravenously, and response is usually seen within 3 months. Common side effects include infusion-related reactions such as nausea, vomiting, headache, and fever. [66] A rare serious side effect of rituximab is progressive multifocal leukoencephalopathy (PML), which has also been seen in other monoclonal antibodies. [67]

agent1_snippets.title: Best Practices For Administering Monoclonal Antibody Therapy For Coronavirus (COVID-19) (Archived) -- Introduction
agent1_snippets.content: Monoclonal antibodies have been identified as a potential therapy to prevent COVID-19 disease progression in patients at risk for severe disease. Most antibodies made by the human body are polyclonal, meaning that they are derived from multiple B lymphocyte lineages and have slightly different specificities for target antigens. Monoclonal antibodies, however, are produced by a single B-lymphocyte clone and are highly specific for their target antigen. [4] [5] Monoclonal antibodies have been in use since 1985 and have been used as therapies for malignancy, autoimmune disease, infectious organisms, and drug reversal. [6] [7] [8]

agent1_snippets.title: Relapsed and Refractory Multiple Myeloma -- Treatment / Management -- Second Relapse or Subsequent Relapse
agent1_snippets.content: In 2022, the FDA approved teclistamab, the first bispecific T-cell engager antibody against CD38 expressed on the myeloma cells, for patients with relapsed or refractory multiple myeloma who have failed 4 prior lines of therapy. Belantamab mafodotin is anti-BCMA humanized immunoglobulin G (IgG) antibody conjugated with monomethyl auristatin. The drug received accelerated approval in the US for patients who had progressed on at least 4 lines of therapy. It is being withdrawn from the market based on the results of randomized phase 3 trials (DREAMM-3), as it did not show clinical benefit compared to pomalidomide and dexamethasone.

agent2_snippets.title: UMLS -- Run 2 -- Level 1 -- Community 214
agent2_snippets.content: Bruton's tyrosine kinase (BTK) inhibitors are classified as protein kinase inhibitors with antineoplastic and immunomodulating activity. Within this class, ibrutinib and zanubrutinib are members; ibrutinib is used to treat chronic lymphocytic leukemia (سرطان الخلايا الليمفاوية المزمن). Epidermal growth factor receptor (EGFR) inhibitors also belong to the broader category of protein kinase inhibitors, antineoplastic and immunomodulating agents. Canertinib dihydrochloride and margetuximab are specific EGFR inhibitors. Other protein kinase inhibitors listed in the Anatomical Therapeutic Chemical (ATC) classification include cabozantinib, gilteritinib, and midostaurin. Gilteritinib and midostaurin are indicated for the treatment of acute myeloid leukemia (سرطان الدم النخاعي الحاد), while cabozantinib is a member of this group with oncologic applications. Collectively, these agents illustrate how diverse protein kinase–targeting drugs—spanning BTK inhibitors, EGFR inhibitors, and other kinase inhibitors—are integrated into cancer therapy, each targeting specific malignancies and contributing to personalized patient care.

agent2_snippets.title: UMLS -- Run 20 -- Level 1 -- Community 70
agent2_snippets.content: BCR‑ABL inhibitors, a subclass of protein kinase inhibitors with antineoplastic and immunomodulating properties, include dasatinib, dasatinib anhydrous, imatinib, ponatinib, ponatinib hydrochloride and asciminib. Dasatinib, dasatinib anhydrous and imatinib are indicated for lymphoid leukemia, whereas ponatinib, ponatinib hydrochloride and asciminib are indicated for myeloid leukemia. Bruton's tyrosine kinase (BTK) inhibitors, also protein kinase inhibitors with antineoplastic and immunomodulating activity, comprise ibrutinib and zanubrutinib; ibrutinib is used to treat chronic lymphocytic leukemia. Epidermal growth factor receptor (EGFR) inhibitors, another protein kinase inhibitor subclass, consist of canertinib dihydrochloride and margetuximab. By blocking EGFR tyrosine‑kinase signaling, these agents disrupt tumor cell proliferation and survival and can modulate immune responses, providing antineoplastic and immunomodulating effects. Collectively, targeted inhibition of BCR‑ABL, BTK, and EGFR exemplifies precision oncology, linking specific molecular targets to distinct hematologic and solid malignancies and guiding patient‑centered cancer therapy.

agent2_snippets.content: The community describes a network of pharmacologic agents and their classifications, therapeutic indications, and contraindications. The ATC category “Other antivirals in ATC” includes a diverse set of antiviral drugs: bulevirtide, maravirok (maraviroc), maribavir, moroxydine, fostemsavir, ibalizumab, and tiloron. Ibalizumab is simultaneously classified as a member of the antiviral ATC group and as a monoclonal antibody; it is explicitly “mapped to” the monoclonal antibody class, confirming its biologic nature.
